h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.anchor_quote: [slides the arrow key next to the marked worksheet] On the key, grass -> rabbit means energy goes from food to eater, so rabbit -> hawk is hawk eating rabbit, not chasing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I can put 2 in both of these, but then it looks like I missed the point of having two labels.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply accept the tutor's explanation and correctly fill in the worksheet blanks.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Now make the row pass a stranger test. Write one short tag under each number.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor invents a new representational and cognitive task to address the learner's persistent discomfort with the identical numbers.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The cover-and-uncover move is done. It proved the bare 2s are ambiguous; it does not prove the worksheet can explain itself without the drawing.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's re-semanticized framing and immediately raises the evidence standard by requiring the row to make sense without the visual aid.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I can put 2 in both of these, but then it looks like I missed the point of having two labels.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The cover-and-uncover move is done. It proved the bare 2s are ambiguous; it does not prove the worksheet can explain itself without the drawing.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's re-semanticized framing and immediately raises the evidence standard by requiring the row to make sense without the visual aid.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Now make the row pass a stranger test. Write one short tag under each number.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor invents a new representational and cognitive task to address the learner's persistent discomfort with the identical numbers.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Now make the row pass a stranger test. Write one short tag under each number.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor invents a new representational and cognitive task to address the learner's persistent discomfort with the identical numbers.

scored.recoheredEarlier: This is double the side, so I still want to call it double.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The old word was doing both jobs: side and area. It hid the second direction.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply compute the area factor for a side factor of 3 by counting grid squares.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the two columns. The old word was trying to do both jobs; the grid will not allow it.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a dual-column representation to structurally resolve the learner's expressed frustration with their initial vocabulary error.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The grid has settled the count; it has not trained the exam sentence. This line is the gate: “If the side factor is ___, the area factor is ___, because ___.”
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor shifts from physical grid counting to formal sentence construction to solidify the learner's newly differentiated understanding of side and area factors.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: This is double the side, so I still want to call it double.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The grid has settled the count; it has not trained the exam sentence. This line is the gate: “If the side factor is ___, the area factor is ___, because ___.”
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor shifts from physical grid counting to formal sentence construction to solidify the learner's newly differentiated understanding of side and area factors.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the two columns. The old word was trying to do both jobs; the grid will not allow it.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a dual-column representation to structurally resolve the learner's expressed frustration with their initial vocabulary error.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the two columns. The old word was trying to do both jobs; the grid will not allow it.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a dual-column representation to structurally resolve the learner's expressed frustration with their initial vocabulary error.
